title: Employ Replay-Resistant Authentication Mechanisms
description: Employ replay-resistant authentication mechanisms for network access to privileged and non-privileged accounts.

checks.name: NTLMv1 is Disabled
checks.purpose: Verify NTLMv1 is disabled via LAN Manager Authentication Level registry setting (must be 3, 4, or 5)

checks.name: Kerberos is the Preferred Authentication Protocol
checks.purpose: Confirm the system is domain joined and uses Kerberos (a nonce-based replay-resistant protocol) for network authentication

checks.name: Windows Hello for Business or Smart Card is Configured
checks.purpose: Verify a phishing-resistant, replay-resistant MFA mechanism (smart card, FIDO2, Windows Hello for Business) is configured

checks.name: NTLMv1 is Disabled
checks.purpose: Verify NTLMv1 is disabled via LAN Manager Authentication Level (must be 3, 4, or 5)

checks.name: Kerberos is the Preferred Authentication Protocol
checks.purpose: Confirm the server is domain joined and Kerberos is used for network authentication by default

checks.name: Extended Protection for Authentication is Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify Extended Protection for Authentication (EPA) is enabled on IIS and LDAP to prevent relay attacks

checks.name: NTLM Restrictions are Enforced via GPO
checks.purpose: Confirm Network Security: Restrict NTLM GPO settings are configured to audit or deny NTLM authentication where Kerberos is available

checks.name: SSH Uses Replay-Resistant Key Exchange Algorithms
checks.purpose: Verify sshd_config restricts KexAlgorithms to modern replay-resistant algorithms (e.g., curve25519-sha256, ecdh-sha2-nistp256)

checks.name: TOTP or Hardware Token MFA is Configured
checks.purpose: Confirm a TOTP or OTP-based MFA mechanism (pam_google_authenticator, pam_oath) is configured, providing time-based one-time passwords that are replay-resistant

checks.name: SSH Protocol 1 is Disabled
checks.purpose: Verify SSH Protocol 1 (vulnerable to replay attacks) is not available or enabled in sshd_config

checks.name: SSH Uses Replay-Resistant Key Exchange Algorithms
checks.purpose: Verify sshd_config restricts KexAlgorithms to modern replay-resistant algorithms on Debian

checks.name: TOTP or Hardware Token MFA is Configured
checks.purpose: Confirm a TOTP or OTP-based MFA mechanism is configured, providing replay-resistant authentication

checks.name: SSH Protocol 1 is Disabled
checks.purpose: Verify SSH Protocol 1 is not available or enabled in sshd_config on Debian

remediation: Disable NTLMv1 by setting LAN Manager Authentication Level to 5 (NTLMv2 only). Enable EPA on IIS and LDAP. Configure GPO to restrict NTLM. Deploy phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2, smart card, Windows Hello). On Linux, restrict SSH KexAlgorithms to modern algorithms and deploy TOTP MFA via pam_google_authenticator.
